sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application 81 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objections 26 & 27. 2. For orders on CMA 876/2024 3. For orders on CMA 877/2024. 4. For hearing of Main Case. 16.12.2025 Mr. S.M. Ali Jafferi, advocate holds brief for Mr. Asad Jehangir Shah, advocate for the applicant. Mr. Irfan Mir Halepota, advocate for respondent. This reference is pending since 2016 without any significant progress, even notices have not been issued. On 01.04.2024 the following order was passed: “1. This matter was taken up in the first round when nobody was present and matter was kept aside. In the second round again no one has turned up. Urgent application is dismissed” Today, merely a brief is being held and adjournment has been sought on behalf of the applicant without any cogent reason or justification whatsoever; therefore, the instant reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution along with pending application(s). A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge M. Khan
